clean_body: | Hello — The situation: National Republicans bought television ad time in Colorado yesterday afternoon. It’s a multi-million dollar ad campaign designed to boost our opponent and falsely attack John’s character in an attempt to reverse our momentum in this battleground Senate race. The problem: This month, our fundraising has slowed down just as these Republican groups started spending virtually unlimited sums of money to do as much damage to our campaign as possible. Our job: These attacks are a huge threat to the Democrats’ best chance of flipping a Senate seat in November, and we have to generate as many grassroots donations as possible to ensure our team has the financial resources to defend John from these false, nasty attacks. The ask: Can John Hickenlooper and our grassroots team count on you to contribute $10 or more this week to help us fight back against this barrage of incoming Republican attacks? This is an important moment for our campaign, and we cannot risk falling short: If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Our thanks: Supporters like you have always stepped up when we needed you most, and we appreciate that so much. We know this is a tough moment for many, so we’re really grateful for anything you can give today. All our best, Team Hick P.S. We know this is a difficult time, so please take care of yourself and your loved ones and only pitch in if you can afford to do so. To pause our emails until this public health emergency has passed, =. Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado Hickenlooper for Colorado PO Box 18886 Denver, CO 80218 United States

name: =?utf-8?Q?Troy=20Downing?=
subject: It’s time to restart the economy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TROY DOWNING FOR MONTANA
body:
|
---
| |
---
| **Dear Friends,

I want to thank you all for your perseverance in, and commitment to, reducing the spread of Covid-19. I am proud of my business partners and team who have helped produce thousands of gallons of hand sanitizer at our distillery and are continuing to help distribute this much-needed protection to our frontlines including first responders and health care providers all over Montana. This has been a big job but it needed to be done. ** **I have personally witnessed so many selfless acts and heard so many stories of my fellow Montanans stepping forward and helping, of giving back to their communities, and I am truly proud of Montana as we battle this uncertainty.** **While we continue to work to flatten the curve and slow the spread of COVID-19, we must now focus on restarting and rebuilding the economy. Without an aggressive plan for economic recovery, many Montana businesses will not survive this pandemic. We must set hard dates to end our quarantine and get people working again and our kids back to school. ** ** I have spent my life building and running businesses in commercial real estate, insurance, investment banking (securities), and manufacturing. I understand first-hand Montana businesses can’t just turn on and off like a light bulb, they need to be primed, pumped, and we need to clear a path free from meaningless regulatory hurdles.

We need to cut red tape and consider waiving many of these regulations that are not directly and demonstrably protecting Montana consumers. To make it easier, I suggest we extend these for at least a year. Uncertainty is an unnecessary burden for Montana business, Montana jobs, and the Montana economy.

We need to look at tax relief for our small businesses to allow them to focus on rebuilding as quickly as possible. We need to encourage Montanans to support Montana’s businesses first. Supporting Montana business, supports Montana workers. Putting Montanans back to work is how we restart the economy. Let’s take care of our own. It will be Montanans who will lift Montanans. **

**As your next State Auditor- Commissioner of Securities and Insurance, and member of the Montana Board of Land Commissioners, I will work to reduce regulations that are roadblocks to business but are not protecting Montana consumers. I will work to increase competition and reduce prices in the insurance industry, I will work to support the backbone of our economy as a thoughtful and informed member of the Land Board, I will work to grow funds for our education system by supporting critical Montana industry.

I have plans to pave the way to a brighter future for us all, but, I’d like to hear from you as well. What can our leaders do that could help your business thrive or give you better job opportunities and help you get back to work? Please send your thoughts and recommendations to Info@TroyDowning.com

We are all in this together. Please stay safe, stay healthy, and let’s get Montana back to work!

May God bless you and yours, and the Great State of Montana.

Troy Downing** ** ** ** **
---
|
---
---

---

name: Jay Inslee
subject: We're all in this together.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D)
body:
We have to have each other's backs right now.
| **From the Desk of Jay Inslee** Friend, The decisions we are making in Washington state are based on one central truth:_We are all in this together._ **Yesterday, I joined my fellow governors in Oregon and California to commit to a shared approach toward reopening our economies that will prioritize residents' health through decisions guided by science. And last week, I announced that the state of Washington will return more than 400 ventilators and that the field hospital at CenturyLink will be dismantled and redeployed so that states facing dire situations -- like New York -- can access the supplies and hospital beds needed to save countless lives.** This fight knows no state or national boundaries._The only way we will defeat this is by working together._ Make no mistake, we are still facing a serious situation here in Washington. But the data shows that our actions to slow the spread of COVID-19 have had a powerful impact. The sacrifices we're all making are working, and this allows us to help other states who need it. We haven't beaten this virus yet. And to do that, all of us are going to have to stay committed to the task before us. Together, we must remain committed to being safe and helping one another. We will continue to stay home, wash our hands, and maintain social distancing. Because of all of us, there's hope, and hope spreads. **We're all in this together.** Very truly yours, Jay
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [Click here to unsubscribe](http://act.jayinslee.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=8049%2E868730%2E6x4YmT). Contributions or gifts to JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D) are not tax deductible. | PAID FOR BY JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D)

name: =?utf-8?Q?Jake=20LaTurner?=
subject: Kansans serving Kansans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kansans for LaTurner
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/9ec1bdd851e1/looking-on-the-bright-side-6860949?e=1050d051da)
---
| | Friend,

Something I was taught as a child has really taken on new meaning in these last few weeks. _When things are tough, look around and find a way to serve._

I have had my team on the lookout lately for people that are doing just that, and they are everywhere. We have nurses and medical personnel, truck drivers and grocery clerks, farmers and ranchers, and many others who continue to suit up and continue to help keep us healthy and fed. Each week [on Facebook](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=a71388c5a6&e=1050d051da), we have been honoring these Kansans. If you have anyone you would like us to feature, [email us ](mailto:team@jakelaturner.com?subject=Kansas%20Helper)a photo and some information about them and we will help honor them.
---
| [ ](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=6358ce3550&e=1050d051da)
---
Jean Pope is a registered nurse who's been working at Stormont Vail Hospital in Topeka for over 20 years. Like Jean, there are many health professionals on the front lines fighting this virus and [I am so grateful for their willingness to keep serving](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=2f67992c0e&e=1050d051da) in hospitals and clinics throughout our state and nation.
| [ ](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=ec27b4e05a&e=1050d051da)
---
Agricultural producers like Robert Jones in rural Kansas continue to do their part by getting out into their fields to ensure we have a plentiful food supply. Feeding the world has never been a small task, and with COVID-19 causing supply shortages across the country, [we continue to be grateful for the work of our farmers and ranchers.](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=ada7b367a8&e=1050d051da)
| [ ](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=98f55a5ca2&e=1050d051da)
---
And then there are those who are out in our communities everyday delivering food, supplies, and essential services that keep our lives going while we all do our best to stay home and stay healthy. Mel Clare from Junction City continues to wake up with a smile and deliver medical supplies to the hospitals, clinics, and pharmacies in his area. [We are grateful for his service and that of the numerous other delivery drivers in our communities right now.](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=504bbeaa47&e=1050d051da)
| I could not be more grateful to live in a state where we care about our neighbors and where people are finding ways to connect with and help one another, even if we can't all do so in the ways we are used to. My heartfelt thanks to those who serve.

May God bless you and your loved ones,

Jake LaTurner
---
|
---
---

subject: National Delegate Application Deadline in One Month!
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www
body:
Applications Due on May 14, 2020
Fellow Democrat,
**Reminder: One Month Left to apply to be a Delegate to the National Convention!**
We know these are unforeen times and we hope you and your loved ones are safe and well. A lot is changing for everyone and while some things are still uncertain, we do not want you to miss your opportunity to run to be a Delegate to the Democratic National Convention.
**Your applications to run for PLEO, District-Level, At-Large, and Alternates are all due on May 14, 2020 to the West Virginia Democratic Party/WVSDEC. **
**Your applications can be found at the following links:**
**The At-Large, District-Level, & Alternate Application may be found here: **[https://wvdemocrats.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/12/National-Convention-Delegate-Candidate-District_At-Large-2020.pdf](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17142880/184090597/11721750?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YTQyNmNiOS1hMzdlLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hUU-ymA4cFFriCpuC1ck-4umYvy1fzvhlh8oJrQs7wg=&emci=09b5c5b6-927e-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=7a426cb9-a37e-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=5094937)

name: Colin Lauderdale
subject: Can you do us a favor?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
Right now, the numbers aren’t close to where we want to be. We’re $4,800 short, but if we get a big push of donations tonight we can close the gap just in time.
#
Hi,
We could really use your help.
Tomorrow marks the mid-way point for April. If we want to stay on top of our monthly expenses, we must hit our $30,000 mid-month goal by tomorrow at midnight.
**Right now, the numbers aren’t close to where we want to be.** We’re $4,800 short, but if we get a big push of donations tonight we can close the gap just in time.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17143057/184093440/-626545291?refcode=em20200414-2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGVlNzNjZi1hNDdlLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=72e5OXSWi3HLPsjtPShINhqlTikzbBKteqlz2FTzaxI=)
**[Will you chip in $4.80 before tomorrow’s midnight deadline? We need everyone who can spare a couple of bucks to help us hit this goal. Every little bit helps in a make or break moment like this.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17143071/184093454/-626545291?refcode=em20200414-2&amount=4.80&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGVlNzNjZi1hNDdlLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=72e5OXSWi3HLPsjtPShINhqlTikzbBKteqlz2FTzaxI=)**
****[Contribute $4.80](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17143072/184093455/-626545291?refcode=em20200414-2&amount=4.80&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGVlNzNjZi1hNDdlLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=72e5OXSWi3HLPsjtPShINhqlTikzbBKteqlz2FTzaxI=)****
Here’s why it’s so critical we hit our mid-month fundraising goal tomorrow night:
1. **At the end of the month, our campaign has a list of bills we need to pay.** There’s website expenses, online video services so we can continue campaigning remotely, phone banking tools, staff salaries, digital ads, and so much more. Hitting our mid-month goal is critical so we can raise enough money by the end of the month to pay our bills.
2. **We have lots of plans for the rest of April, but we’re not sure what we can afford until we see how our fundraising numbers look.** Our team has come up with lots of ideas to build out our grassroots strategy, energize voters in Kentucky, and grow a people-powered movement strong enough to defeat Mitch. But first, we need to make sure we can afford them.

subject: Fox guarding the hen house
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sánchez
body:
The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) recently announced it would drastically loosen environmental protections _due to coronavirus_. **Not only is that nonsensical, but it 's also _dangerous_.**
According to the _New York Times_ , the EPA will allow "power plants, factories and other facilities to determine _for themselves_ if they are able to meet legal requirements on reporting air and water pollution."
Using a global health crisis as a guise to loosen environmental protections is a new low for the Trump Administration. [**If you, like Linda, expect the EPA to _do its job_ and protect our air and waterways, SIGN our petition>>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/MA4VQAkaoWthMGwf6aEqqkIFxQNX9bf5dFlWiGpLorM=/funO5jnC36t3Z6cbVgLGNfbXtDawfhVXVQrcOOmS05U=)
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/MA4VQAkaoWthMGwf6aEqqkIFxQNX9bf5dFlWiGpLorM=/faJnGo6BxULC4f27oPuh9OzUmMZEUwBsNAbEXobathY=)
To think the EPA is _loosening_ _regulations on air quality_ because of a global disease that is _more likely_ _to affect people with_ _respiratory issues_ is incredibly short-sighted and unfounded.
**On top of that, asking corporate polluters to regulate themselves is like asking the fox to guard the hen house.** We have the EPA for a _reason: _to ensure our natural resources are _protected_ and _safe_.
[**Will you demand the EPA enforce environmental protections by signing on?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/MA4VQAkaoWthMGwf6aEqqkIFxQNX9bf5dFlWiGpLorM=/funO5jnC36t3Z6cbVgLGNfbXtDawfhVXVQrcOOmS05U=)

Thank you for taking action,
\-- _Team S anchez_

name: Team Cotton
subject: How to Make China Pay
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cotton for Senate
body:
While the liberal media spreads propaganda from the Chinese Communist Party, Tom has been fighting back. Check out Tom's latest Fox News editorial outlining how to make China pay for its role spreading the China virus.
Click here to [unsubscribe](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/servlet/OneClickOptOutServlet?m=42261405&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MTQ5NTM1NgS2&mt=1&rt=0)

Peter,

While the liberal media spreads propaganda from the Chinese Communist Party, Tom has been fighting back.

Check out Tom's latest Fox News editorial outlining how to make China pay for its role spreading the China virus.

Tom has been under attack from the liberal media for speaking the truth about China, [**can you chip in $10, $25, $50 or even $100 to help fight back?**](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=NDIyNjE0MDUS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MTQ5NTM1NgS2&mt=1&rt=0)

-Team Cotton

[**How to Make China 's Communist Party Pay for the Wuhan Virus **](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=NDIyNjE0MDUS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MTQ5NTM1NgS2&mt=1&rt=0)

_The world is now in the midst of a pandemic that threatens millions. Our first priority in this emergency is suppressing the coronavirus to keep Americans safe. But our next priority must be holding the Chinese Communist Party accountable for unleashing this plague on the world.

Instead of reporting the deadly outbreak in Wuhan when it first appeared, the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) engaged in a cover-up, intimidating brave whistleblowers like the late Dr. Li Wenliang, destroying samples that pointed to the virus's origin, and shutting down a laboratory that shared the virus's genetic sequence on the Internet.

By the time the CCP switched from cover-up to containment, it was already too late for the Chinese people and the world. Officials resorted to draconian measures like welding shut apartment doors in a desperate bid to control the virus's spread.

Just because the CCP's tardy efforts failed doesn't mean a pandemic was inevitable. According to researchers at the University of Southampton, worldwide cases could've been reduced by two-thirds if the CCP had responded to the virus just one week sooner; if it had responded three weeks sooner, a stunning 95 percent of cases could've been avoided.
Instead of fighting the virus to save its citizens, the CCP fought a P.R. battle to save its own skin while enlisting its cronies at the World Health Organization to toe the Party line.

Months later, the CCP is still engaged in a propaganda war about the coronavirus. Last month, a Chinese foreign ministry spokesman speculated that the U.S. Army might have brought the virus to Wuhan.

His comments are part of a larger misinformation campaign to shift the blame for this pandemic from Beijing, where it belongs, to literally anyone else.

We shouldn't allow the CCP to escape blame for causing this pandemic nor should we put ourselves in a position to be victimized by it again.

That means, first, we should treat this pandemic not just as a war with a deadly pathogen but as an information war with the CCP.

This campaign can start by ignoring cynical CCP flacks and politically correct dupes who claim that referring to the China virus by its point of origin is somehow xenophobic or racist.

The United States must also undertake an investigation into which Communist officials are responsible for covering up the outbreak in Wuhan. This investigation should lead to stiff sanctions against the officials we identify. I introduced a bill last week to make such sanctions possible.

Furthermore, we should restrict Chinese propaganda organs that sow misinformation within the United States, like state-sponsored newspaper China Daily and Confucius Centers on college campuses.

We can even rename the street outside the CCP's embassy in Washington, D.C. after whistleblowers like Dr. Li, so that the CCP's functionaries are forced to confront their party's victims every day when they arrive at work.

As we make sure the history books record the facts about the China virus pandemic, we must also make sure the CCP pays a price for its malpractice, here and now.

If this pandemic has proven anything, it's that the CCP has abandoned any claim to global leadership. We should move immediately to demote China from positions of prominence in international institutions like the United Nations as well as technical standards-setting bodies that Beijing has coopted in recent years to promote Chinese technology.

We can also bring action against China at the World Trade Organization and revoke its special trading privileges with our country for threatening to cut off access to vital supplies in a time of crisis.

Likewise, Beijing's influence at the WHO should be rooted out. Cronies like WHO official Bruce Aylward ought to be replaced with impartial public servants who are willing to buck the party line in Beijing.

We should also seek membership in the WHO for Taiwan. It has handled the pandemic better than perhaps any country to date, despite its shameful exclusion from the WHO at China's insistence.

Perhaps the strongest rebuke we could deliver to the CCP, however, is to end our dependence on China for essential goods like medicine, respirator masks, and other strategically important goods. Export-oriented manufacturing is one source of the CCP's leverage over foreign countries, allowing it to cut off its trading partners in times of emergency. Pulling our most sensitive supply chains out of China must therefore be a top priority.

That's why I have a bill, along with Rep. Mike Gallagher, R-Wisc., to end our dependence on Chinese drugs and encourage companies to make essential medicine in America.
Competing with China in the long run will require us to emancipate ourselves from Chinese supply chains for a broader category of high-value goods, while rebuilding our manufacturing capabilities at home so we can quickly surge production of needed supplies.

Times of crisis are painful but they're also clarifying. At home, this pandemic has allowed our brave doctors and nurses to prove that they're the best medical professionals in the world. Tragically, it has also exposed alarming vulnerabilities in our government's response to emergencies.

Abroad, this crisis has proven that Chinese communism is a grave affliction whose pain is felt not only by its subjects but by the whole world.

We must treat the CCP as we would any serious disease: immediately and aggressively._

| |

| Paid for by Cotton for Senate
---

2226 Cottondale Lane
Suite 200
Little Rock, AR 72202

_Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army. Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or any service branch._

Check out Tom's latest Fox News editorial outlining how to make China pay for its role spreading the China virus. Tom has been under attack from the liberal media for speaking the truth about China, -Team Cotton The world is now in the midst of a pandemic that threatens millions. Our first priority in this emergency is suppressing the coronavirus to keep Americans safe. But our next priority must be holding the Chinese Communist Party accountable for unleashing this plague on the world. Instead of reporting the deadly outbreak in Wuhan when it first appeared, the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) engaged in a cover-up, intimidating brave whistleblowers like the late Dr. Li Wenliang, destroying samples that pointed to the virus's origin, and shutting down a laboratory that shared the virus's genetic sequence on the Internet. By the time the CCP switched from cover-up to containment, it was already too late for the Chinese people and the world. Officials resorted to draconian measures like welding shut apartment doors in a desperate bid to control the virus's spread. Just because the CCP's tardy efforts failed doesn't mean a pandemic was inevitable. According to researchers at the University of Southampton, worldwide cases could've been reduced by two-thirds if the CCP had responded to the virus just one week sooner; if it had responded three weeks sooner, a stunning 95 percent of cases could've been avoided. Instead of fighting the virus to save its citizens, the CCP fought a P.R. battle to save its own skin while enlisting its cronies at the World Health Organization to toe the Party line. Months later, the CCP is still engaged in a propaganda war about the coronavirus. Last month, a Chinese foreign ministry spokesman speculated that the U.S. Army might have brought the virus to Wuhan. His comments are part of a larger misinformation campaign to shift the blame for this pandemic from Beijing, where it belongs, to literally anyone else. We shouldn't allow the CCP to escape blame for causing this pandemic nor should we put ourselves in a position to be victimized by it again. That means, first, we should treat this pandemic not just as a war with a deadly pathogen but as an information war with the CCP. This campaign can start by ignoring cynical CCP flacks and politically correct dupes who claim that referring to the China virus by its point of origin is somehow xenophobic or racist. The United States must also undertake an investigation into which Communist officials are responsible for covering up the outbreak in Wuhan. This investigation should lead to stiff sanctions against the officials we identify. I introduced a bill last week to make such sanctions possible. Furthermore, we should restrict Chinese propaganda organs that sow misinformation within the United States, like state-sponsored newspaper China Daily and Confucius Centers on college campuses. We can even rename the street outside the CCP's embassy in Washington, D.C. after whistleblowers like Dr. Li, so that the CCP's functionaries are forced to confront their party's victims every day when they arrive at work. As we make sure the history books record the facts about the China virus pandemic, we must also make sure the CCP pays a price for its malpractice, here and now. If this pandemic has proven anything, it's that the CCP has abandoned any claim to global leadership. We should move immediately to demote China from positions of prominence in international institutions like the United Nations as well as technical standards-setting bodies that Beijing has coopted in recent years to promote Chinese technology. "Joe has the character and the experience to guide us through one of our darkest times."
#
_Peter, we hope you are staying safe and healthy during these trying times. We wanted to share a some exciting news from this morning - President Barack Obama endorsed Joe Biden for President!_
_Here are a few ways you can help get the word out: _
* * *
**Watch & Share**
President Obama says Joe Biden is what so many others pretend to be: a statesman. Check out the video below, then share it with your friends on social media.
[](https://act.unitethecountry.com/page/m/46df763f/287b9173/2b058b0b/7e76766f/2769616106/VEsH/?g=x7_OMVH8x8VdxQ307JVTQtA "https://pic.twitter.com/DWxPXuNKbL")

**[WATCH ON FACEBOOK](https://act.unitethecountry.com/page/m/46df763f/287b9173/2b058b0b/7e767668/2769616106/VEsF/?g=x7_OMVH8x8VdxQ307JVTQtA "https://www.facebook.com/UniteTheCountryPAC/videos/237591707481867/")**

**Share**

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Answering your questions about cloth masks
body:
Your daily Rhode Island COVID-19 update
---
| | | | | |
---
Hello friend,
Rhode Islanders want to know how to best protect themselves and their families from the coronavirus while out in public. Over the last few weeks, we've taken steps to reduce the crowds in large essential retailers, and we've directed stores to make sure people are social distancing in lines. But we've also recommended that all Rhode Islanders wear cloth face coverings when out and about. People understandably have questions about this policy, and I'd like to clarify what we mean.
First, I'm sad to report another 7 deaths a result of coronavirus for a total of 80. We have 275 new cases in Rhode Island for a total of 3,251. We have 213 people in the hospital. [These statistics are always available to you online from the Rhode Island Department of Health](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014tN13TYsWDQLoSWjVyLZx0KuTpmZRq189GkhgPWBBexcYL55Lsj3EPEqbj7CeV0FA0v4RbSjBygw-DSJ2L4zj5T8LR_JwrH6v9AKYflN8Tcj31ipCdVjq8exXmfgxYBsaZsLBRPCri81W_OXbZIgmkmpQlVVfCg3HUPqPb4vaVQ=&c=8A6fh8OjlYOIrxKoAanM8L6aB7KQBQpfyncNhxgyc-hX3RPpw4vq2w==&ch=cI1OjIjGDkOqcFwwpw-DpF6nU5XVgat9_FMyQvmhE4o_ysrDUv8glA==).
---
[ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014tN13TYsWDQLoSWjVyLZx0KuTpmZRq189GkhgPWBBexcYL55Lsj3EHV-eBIugRqIgq0tdt63UyQoGh7ID4ZJxdMjhy_7NAq_vGUTwOwrfuP0ykRzf5lDGX9MDkI6UqtFmICoBydFosIRk4N4RLWO09IirJRdldziO3TTeUrur57SrHGg_IKVx6ds0LIWU639rFGXgpBAEKgZp9hEPzHvLazEcfl0EnjJ&c=8A6fh8OjlYOIrxKoAanM8L6aB7KQBQpfyncNhxgyc-hX3RPpw4vq2w==&ch=cI1OjIjGDkOqcFwwpw-DpF6nU5XVgat9_FMyQvmhE4o_ysrDUv8glA==)
---
| Click to watch today's update on Rhode Island's COVID-19 response
---
| Last week we began recommending that we should all be wearing cloth face coverings when we are out in public. Today, I signed an [executive order](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014tN13TYsWDQLoSWjVyLZx0KuTpmZRq189GkhgPWBBexcYL55Lsj3EHV-eBIugRqIijSEeK5v1KMbz89QqapEDAe1OGoN4uxbTuMnSMp0ufdlXV31o36i988i-uo01f8Ddx6x-OChRRpGOjqAHrIGNf-PiWebIA4sqTgMYgozTwWqKsvfzNDc0aI8GvTEzk6w7G9xvfPof-s=&c=8A6fh8OjlYOIrxKoAanM8L6aB7KQBQpfyncNhxgyc-hX3RPpw4vq2w==&ch=cI1OjIjGDkOqcFwwpw-DpF6nU5XVgat9_FMyQvmhE4o_ysrDUv8glA==) making this direction clearer.
Beginning Saturday:

* All employees of customer-facing businesses, office-based businesses and nonprofits must wear cloth face coverings when they are at work.
* All of these businesses must provide face coverings for their employees.
* Additionally, all customer-facing businesses must take steps to require customers to wear face coverings. That means they should be putting up signs at the door reminding customers to wear a face covering inside.

The only exceptions from these rules are for anyone whose health would be in jeopardy because of wearing a face covering or any children under 2 years old.
If you're someone who is seeing more people wearing masks and you're wondering: Do I really have to do this? The answer is yes, you have to do it. While it may be uncomfortable, none of the new rules we're dealing with are comfortable for any of us. We're doing it because this is what we have to do to keep each other safe.
If you're a business or an individual and you need help figuring out how to make your own mask at home, you can go to the [Rhode Island Commerce website](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014tN13TYsWDQLoSWjVyLZx0KuTpmZRq189GkhgPWBBexcYL55Lsj3EHV-eBIugRqIzOGiZuhmTUiEsL_SlWvSY33I7OTKWoif333ZwyV_QKjMLkY93JCjj0xggGY3uvlO8kEbLOU65tboKpIJnuMY8A==&c=8A6fh8OjlYOIrxKoAanM8L6aB7KQBQpfyncNhxgyc-hX3RPpw4vq2w==&ch=cI1OjIjGDkOqcFwwpw-DpF6nU5XVgat9_FMyQvmhE4o_ysrDUv8glA==) for some helpful videos and tips.
This is an important change to keep safe the workers who are keeping our economy open, but it's not a silver bullet. We still have to stay vigilant with our social distancing and ensure that no one is going to work sick. You've often heard me say that every Rhode Islanders should have affordable, quality health insurance. That's especially true at a time like this, which is why HealthSource RI is extending a special enrollment period through April 30 to allow Rhode Islanders to purchase coverage.
If you are interested in getting health insurance through HSRI, you can call them at (855) 840-4774 or [visit their website](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014tN13TYsWDQLoSWjVyLZx0KuTpmZRq189GkhgPWBBexcYL55Lsj3EHV-eBIugRqIx-y0rZZv7-yxBvlzQF02SxqZpjhD727l0aSIJ-Yzqmuk8uIwxTt-gIQCNEJQ1dN70lm5KeY5jjwiMjIVCztBeQ==&c=8A6fh8OjlYOIrxKoAanM8L6aB7KQBQpfyncNhxgyc-hX3RPpw4vq2w==&ch=cI1OjIjGDkOqcFwwpw-DpF6nU5XVgat9_FMyQvmhE4o_ysrDUv8glA==) to learn more.
We're all in this together.
-Gina Raimondo Governor
P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19, If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use [this link](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014tN13TYsWDQLoSWjVyLZx0KuTpmZRq189GkhgPWBBexcYL55Lsj3EHV-eBIugRqIjqrLMNeof9uTO0Qwfj2CKmvAB2OjzIsPE_yp63EsuEseJo2-WLDSPfOHgPSgb24WUbR4WUuCIhbDxb2JtvNR2cTb5rxM81gwNL5pn4BmI7Lq_4cNTMmhixSGVLkr7SxF61TsQ_SXozZBIre3lEPfH3rQ2ufee5DVmyFUNFSjOqDJY276jcqKR5cdkz7TPxC2NtL7ohWb49JNWd5vUaKITo4HR1fpDLseg8DtGh7iASLAUrTRT9KL7R_yI3JR-CVZhTMrvl6slmY=&c=8A6fh8OjlYOIrxKoAanM8L6aB7KQBQpfyncNhxgyc-hX3RPpw4vq2w==&ch=cI1OjIjGDkOqcFwwpw-DpF6nU5XVgat9_FMyQvmhE4o_ysrDUv8glA==).
---
| | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903
---
| [Unsubscribe dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=un&m=001_rvij1smacIHQplL2vH8YA%3D&ch=e9e38b2e-7e90-11ea-b887-d4ae5292c4bc&ca=8bcdd57a-4e1f-4d58-b857-f4198f5bc8f4)
---
[Update Profile](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=oo&m=001_rvij1smacIHQplL2vH8YA%3D&ch=e9e38b2e-7e90-11ea-b887-d4ae5292c4bc&ca=8bcdd57a-4e1f-4d58-b857-f4198f5bc8f4) | [About Constant Contact](http://www.constantcontact.com/legal/about-constant-contact)
Sent by [communications@governor.ri.gov](mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov) in collaboration with
| [ ](http://www.constantcontact.com/index.jsp?cc=nge&rmc=VF19_3GE)
---
[Try email marketing for free today!](http://www.constantcontact.com/index.jsp?cc=nge&rmc=VF19_3GE)

He led the charge to take back the House for the American people and Democrats (and succeeded by a historic margin) and now he's running to take back the Senate too.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because Ben Ray isn't taking a cent of corporate PAC money. His campaign is 100% people-powered. Stand with Mike Turner to defend this key seat.

Nancy Pelosi has officially labeled Mike’s seat as a top target on her list of districts to flip in 2020.

Peter,

Nancy Pelosi is furious that Mike Turner has continued to stand up to her in Congress, so she’s now having her national campaign arm target his seat in November’s election!

Nancy Pelosi has officially labeled Mike’s seat as a top target on her list of districts to flip in 2020. It’s up to all of us to stand together with Mike so that he can continue to defend and protect Ohio, Dayton, and the Miami Valley into 2021.

We can’t let Pelosi win! Will you add your name today and stand with Mike Turner so that he can continue to stand up to her? http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=NDIyNjMwMTgS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MTUwNjQ1OQS2&mt=1&rt=0

name: Megan West
subject: Thursday: LIVE Conversation with Abby Johnson
body:
| | Streaming LIVE This Thursday
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iXf3gw0SBkD1n0daNPbuHzTaHj0Mb_nvECtkIXngmd6T&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200414com01&n=1)
| Peter, The coronavirus is impacting everyone in various ways. Most of us are ordered to “shelter-in-place” and non-essential businesses have been closed in an effort to help _save lives_. But the abortion industry is fighting hard to maintain that abortion is an “essential health service,” pushing for abortion accessibility in every state even though the procedure itself _takes a life_. Abby Johnson, former Planned Parenthood director turned pro-life advocate, will join us LIVE to talk about the state of abortion in this coronavirus age and the tremendous opportunity we each have to do something to protect life, as the need is growing. **Join us, this Thursday, April 16, at 4:30 PM (ET), 1:30 PM (PT) for a LIVE Conversation with Abby Johnson.** RSVP to the LIVE event [here](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iXf3gw0SBkD1n0daNPbuHzThPWpvPCyMwA7jvmH-hY_P44hWW7eXEuXwN-j6uYqsOeTrfvx2c7w9NAW3aiMGZAmLTZdxl4SQvuQeHwTQ1tUZ-TAWf-L42ySUvaytbP-w4A&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200414com01&n=2). It will be streamed LIVE on both Facebook and YouTube. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iXf3gw0SBkD1n0daNPbuHzThPWpvPCyMwA7jvmH-hY_P44hWW7eXEuXwN-j6uYqsOeTrfvx2c7w9NAW3aiMGZAmLTZdxl4SQvuQeHwTQ1tUZ-TAWf-L42ySUvaytbP-w4A&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200414com01&n=3) **Pray. Think. Vote.** Megan West
Media Director
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200414com01&n=4)

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: TOMORROW (Wednesday): Coronavirus tele-town hall
body:
| [](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15479.15827564.4541619.7517223&report_id=Van+Hollen+Live+Events&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fvanhollen.senate.gov%2flive&redir_log=470680465013234 "Van Hollen Live Events") Tomorrow (Wednesday) at 2:10PM ET, I'm hosting a Telephone Townhall with Dr. Josh Sharfstein, a public health expert, Vice Dean at Johns Hopkins, and former Maryland Secretary of Health and Health Commissioner for Baltimore City.

Join us to hear updates on our response to COVID-19 and hear more about what still needs to be done - both to stop the spread and to help Marylanders weathering economic challenges right now.

You can sign up to join the call on my website, or stream it at [vanhollen.senate.gov/live](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15479.15827564.4541619.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fvanhollen.senate.gov%2flive&redir_log=470680465013234). I hope you'll be there.

Chris Van Hollen
---

body:

|

| | We have exciting news everyone! We're almost ready to release our new TV ad. As you read this, our media team is putting the finishing touches on it. This is a big moment for us in this campaign, and we need every single voter to see just why Admiral Mike is the right choice to be our next senator. We will be releasing our ad early to a select group of supporters -- including you -- this week. name: Claudia Tenney
subject: re: these 3 are doing great things
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Claudia for Congress |
body:
I’ve never been more proud to be a part of this community. Our businesses, even the ones struggling right now, have stepped up to support neighbors, [ ](http://links.claudiaforcongress.com/u/click?_t=e8d915629893476cb837806f005d02de&_m=cfdc89bece5b42b5ae2a6799aa4bd667&_e=Ix3Tda_4V3xCkevLKsAJ_LwvXVWS0UENonY-4rkAhr03Q__UTfePw7AILVm8yvdmaBBYfQ1msjfgfMASnnOyRD8cZ6l4pr34wocE1QJwstv-FRnlRfYItws1E3Ss0wYByl_xeA2bUYn8pWnij1oS3Drdi-O-hIOZf-feHyH7mBH8jLB0vEgfAbxGMMv7UJJtt7VeWZvPAsIp3DA31wlkls7T_MjE16338PFY2ItytGUOuGXlHjmSoXT6JTotzpOBKnj3s8h27V-hzjtOAXcI0O2GFmbPFbQ8wHZaymuvd5Gl8MhL2D67obikfPRWdkc_g1O-EBXEIKMsbK5-hvNxUYEuOUGWdTuhYcpMiisbRfshzubLXHE84GcTvQwGnXIGuNysCwdkQCkfo8mKT8VOTwAoge4l444N_-bORrkRErFwMrWHCp7u1kctVA1KngsMPLNdDW3arXSF1RMvHeG0L9_bQIz4DEGQRNJwRTnjt1g%3D) Friend, our community is coming together in this crisis and supporting each other - just like we should. I’ve never been more proud to be a part of this community. Our businesses, even the ones struggling right now, have stepped up to support neighbors, healthcare workers, and families, to keep Upstate New York running.
As promised, I want to highlight those inspiring members of the business community. There are a lot! But this week, I want to highlight THREE amazing Binghamton restaurants who are providing FREE MEALS to kids in NY-22.
* **Lost Dog Cafe**
* **Peterson's Tavern**
* **The Belmar**

As a big THANK YOU, our small business initiative is helping these great businesses and workers by encouraging members of our community to order take out, or buy a gift card for a nurse or doctor, local police, or an EMT, or your local grocer.
**You can support Lost Dog Cafe, Peterson’s Tavern, and the Belmar, along with dozens more small businesses who are giving back at this link:** **[https://claudiaforcongress.com/support-local-businesses/](http://links.claudiaforcongress.com/u/click?_t=e8d915629893476cb837806f005d02de&_m=cfdc89bece5b42b5ae2a6799aa4bd667&_e=Ix3Tda_4V3xCkevLKsAJ_LwvXVWS0UENonY-4rkAhr03Q__UTfePw7AILVm8yvdmaBBYfQ1msjfgfMASnnOyRD8cZ6l4pr34wocE1QJwstv-FRnlRfYItws1E3Ss0wYByl_xeA2bUYn8pWnij1oS3Drdi-O-hIOZf-feHyH7mBH8jLB0vEgfAbxGMMv7UJJtt7VeWZvPAsIp3DA31wlkls7T_MjE16338PFY2ItytGUOuGXlHjmSoXT6JTotzpOBKnj3s8h27V-hzjtOAXcI0LKfgbSGTT3O2G4979G4AJHIGbxTnNDHsVdQqTanrN7969p71ks5OX-B3PA-i-bZD3cI6hpbqOnOPk4BvUmqGjl0ly-21D7wC_74Q5F-a-9-Zjc5pCZYz1BAJTlv8BQKH5uE5mCrXEKXIPtUBlp3rf3-L9fjoJo9NbORgwt0ctQjuk2ZC-2oT6IL20hcsN50iAXM3Wj2OkU5wP5CozrWFOU%3D)**
If you can take a moment today, make sure you recognize their hard work and support of our families in need. While they’re supporting us - I want to make sure we support them, too. [ **SUPPORT SMALL BUSINESS** ](http://links.claudiaforcongress.com/u/click?_t=e8d915629893476cb837806f005d02de&_m=cfdc89bece5b42b5ae2a6799aa4bd667&_e=Ix3Tda_4V3xCkevLKsAJ_LwvXVWS0UENonY-4rkAhr03Q__UTfePw7AILVm8yvdmaBBYfQ1msjfgfMASnnOyRD8cZ6l4pr34wocE1QJwstv-FRnlRfYItws1E3Ss0wYByl_xeA2bUYn8pWnij1oS3Drdi-O-hIOZf-feHyH7mBH8jLB0vEgfAbxGMMv7UJJtt7VeWZvPAsIp3DA31wlkls7T_MjE16338PFY2ItytGUOuGXlHjmSoXT6JTotzpOBKnj3s8h27V-hzjtOAXcI0Lf_L_N7DHNPD673-GzfKIEKLrywbbX-RwgBfLYCPt21WEbHTkR6s2puejG1dyLvmrMXL5P6s933e9YoEHHtm-0NChHkNBYj70RVp31Z5xf4ZteAi0-unI1-RvhvoNI8C4KGM0hFca0H47w_luOJQxTcUT6Q6L4KtkSpVIJeAnxQW81iTi1N2Rl9D8JNJZ-EsGE75XfXaHYZ7OK7C8lEQcs%3D) Let’s keep working together to make life better in this difficult time. -Claudia Tenney |

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump meets with Americans who recovered from Coronavirus
body:
“Thank you for everything that you have done,” state Rep. Karen Whitsett—a Democrat from Michigan who recently battled Coronavirus—told the President. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=131cfb38f7&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • April 14, 2020
---
| | |
# **President Trump meets with Americans who recovered from Coronavirus**

**This afternoon,** President Trump met with former Coronavirus patients who have fully recuperated from the illness. He invited them to the White House to share their recovery stories with the American people.

**“Thank you** for everything that you have done,” state Rep. Karen Whitsett—a Democrat from Michigan who recently battled Coronavirus—told the President and Vice President.

🎬 [Rep. Whitsett, a former Coronavirus patient, shares her story.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=367c2f8c22&e=3ee4985eea)

**At yesterday’s** White House briefing, President Trump updated Americans on encouraging signs of progress in our nation’s battle against the deadly disease.

**“Over the weekend** , the number of daily new infections remained flat nationwide—flat. Hospitalizations are slowing in hotspots like New York, New Jersey, Michigan, and Louisiana,” he said.

**Because Americans** have carefully heeded the President’s [Coronavirus Guidelines](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e04d55b132&e=3ee4985eea), we are beating this invisible enemy. The next step, President Trump says, is preparing to reopen our economy and getting more people back to work once it’s safe.

**“We will soon** finalize new and very important guidelines to give governors the information they need to start safely opening their states,” he said. “My administration’s plan and corresponding guidelines will give the American people the confidence they need to begin returning to normal life.”

🎬 [President Trump: We’re winning our battle against the Coronavirus](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1fb1ea4e1e&e=3ee4985eea)

**WATCH** : [“We're very close to completing a plan to open our country.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5f2852c4f0&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Video of the day: First Lady’s message for children**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e45014a5aa&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **During this difficult time** in the world**,** First Lady Melania Trump recorded a special message for America’s children—many of whom are separated from school, friends, and extended family right now.

**“I encourage you** to read books, continue to learn, help with chores, and share some laughter with your family,” she said.

**Today, she** **also unveiled** some new [Activities for Kids at Home](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5e37419bb0&e=3ee4985eea) as a part of her “Be Best” initiative for children. The new activities include a [Be Best puzzle](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9b7f619a80&e=3ee4985eea), [word searches](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d82064d7c1&e=3ee4985eea), and a worksheet about [White House fun facts](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f1a17edaf0&e=3ee4985eea).

**“We are thinking** of you, and we are all in this together.”
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day
---
|
---
| **President Trump and Vice President Pence meet with patients who have recovered from Coronavirus** | April 14, 2020
---
|

body:

* Flew search and rescue missions in Afghanistan, earning a Purple Heart and Distinguished Flying Cross with Valor for her heroism after the Taliban shot down her helicopter
* Took on the Pentagon to open ground combat roles to women, advocating on _both_ sides of the aisle
* Raising two young boys as a working mom after life-threatening pregnancies -- **so you can count on MJ to protect and improve our health care**

---

**And here 's what Sen. Cornyn has been up to during the same time:**
* Raising tons of money from private detention companies, Big Pharma, and the gun lobby
* Trying to repeal health care protections for Texans -- _twenty times_
* Giving the White House the green light to confiscate private land from Texans to build Trump's border wall

---
We need someone in the Senate who knows what life is like for working Texans -- not someone who's spent 18 years selling out Texans to the special interests. We need MJ.
